free_text: A first target of 400 mu-g/cm2 of isotopically-enriched
76Ge deposited on 10 mg/cm2 of 208Pb, to ensure that
all recoiling 77Ge ions were stopped in the backing.
Second target was 400 mu-g/cm2 of isotopically-enriched
76Ge deposited on carbon foil 20 mu-g/cm2 thick, with
the carbon facing the beam, to allow recoiling ions
to enter into the Fragment Mass Analyzer.
Sample was positioned in the center of
Fragment Mass Analyzer is a triplefocussing
recoil mass spectrometer. It uses an achromatic
symmetric  combination of two electric dipoles and one
magnetic dipole to separate nuclear reaction recoils
from the primary beam and quadrupole doublets at the
entrance and exit of the device for geometric focussing
